item.title: Prepare for a perilous summer in markets
item.description: Potential hotspots include a reformist new Fed chair, weak Japanese yen and high-stakes earnings season

item.title: What’s really going on with mental health?
item.description: Changing terminology risks misleading policymakers and harming those in most need

item.title: Jersey Mike’s IPO tries to sprinkle SpaceX magic on a sandwich
item.description: Sandwich chain’s float a test of appetite for a sector pinched by concerns over consumer spending

item.title: The long electoral lineage of Count Binface
item.description: Parody candidates are a well-established tradition in Britain’s political panto

item.title: You don’t need to pay through the nose to enjoy travel
item.description: One of my favourite travel memories cost less than €100

item.title: Rewiring the nation, Burnham-style
item.description: Collaboration is the secret sauce that will make the proposed new way of governing the UK work

item.title: The Goldilocks zone of messiness
item.description: Tourists flock to Japan to bask in the country’s orderliness. But has a refusal to tolerate chaos killed its capacity for risk?

item.title: Why the chips are down despite the AI boom
item.description: Some believe the boom-bust cycle in memory chips has ended — the market thinks otherwise

item.title: A travel hack’s travel hacks
item.description: Seven tips from the road — by the FT’s Travel Editor

item.title: Is it the last post for postcards?
item.description: Once a delight to find among the bills and junk mail on your doormat, these days it is hard to find one to actually send

item.title: How to find a restaurant on holiday
item.description: Swerve the tourist traps this summer with Marina O’Loughlin’s tried-and-tested tips

item.title: Margaret’s, Cambridge: comfort with excellent tailoring — restaurant review
item.description: This relaxed offshoot of Michelin-starred Twenty-Two is generous, easy and quietly special

item.title: The science of making bordeaux better
item.description: From softer tannins to a fresher style, researchers and winemakers are reshaping one of wine’s most traditional regions

item.title: Billionaire Xavier Niel could be the jolt Vodafone needs
item.description: Investors hope £4.4bn move for telecoms group augurs quicker and sharper improvements

item.title: The superpower of public equity
item.description: Liquidity is tempting companies back to the public markets — but it is not available to all

item.title: Apollo’s profitability challenge: turn easyJet into Ryanair
item.description: If the PE firm wants to raise revenue and cut costs at the airline, there’s room for manoeuvre

item.title: Mass immigration is not the silver bullet economists think it is
item.description: As with the free-trade debate in recent years, consensus is shifting

item.title: Nato’s carnival of Trumpian caprice
item.description: Alliance summits have become an exercise in managing an erratic US president

item.title: Don’t underestimate Farage in this Very British Farce
item.description: The ‘establishment’ he is so fond of bashing must beware of playing into stereotype

item.title: Iran is seeing through Trump’s bluff
item.description: The US president finds himself stuck in a cul-de-sac of his own making

item.title: What’s the etiquette for a WhatsApp street group?
item.description: Democracy or dictatorship, these are the rules of engagement

item.title: Erika McEntarfer: ‘You really can just fire the BLS commissioner’
item.description: The former labour statistics chief on the fragility (and strength) of US economic data

item.title: How AI changes the rules of engagement for sports viewers
item.description: Spectators will see more opportunity to move from pricey subscriptions and rigid schedules to more personalised feeds

item.title: SK Hynix’s jumbo share sale a sign of overheated times
item.description: Perhaps it will be some comfort to those with longer time horizons that market anomalies don’t last for ever

item.title: Why AI could be a financial ‘sludge’ buster
item.description: Regulators want to use the technology in their bid to cut red tape
